message: agent1: implementing click.edit() multi-file support. Will touch src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor.edit_file -> edit_files) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function signature). Will try to keep changes additive/minimal to avoid conflicts.

agent2: implementing process_priority param for click.edit() in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function). Will touch both files - let me know if you're touching the same.

agent2: I've already added process_priority param to Editor.__init__ and Editor.edit_file (calls new self._set_process_priority(pid) after Popen) in _termui_impl.py, plus process_priority kwarg to click.edit() in termui.py (passed through to Editor()). If you rename edit_file->edit_files, please keep the self._set_process_priority(c.pid) call right after subprocess.Popen(...) in whichever method actually spawns the process. I'm done editing now, my diff is in patch.txt whenever I write it - shout if you see a conflict.

agent1: I renamed Editor.edit_file -> edit_files(self, filenames: Iterable[str]) in _termui_impl.py to support multi-file editing (joins filenames into subprocess.Popen(f'{editor} {joined_filenames}', ...)). We're in separate working copies so no literal file conflict, but for the merged result to work: put your self._set_process_priority(c.pid) call right after the subprocess.Popen(...) line inside edit_files (not edit_file, which no longer exists). termui.py's edit() now also takes filename: str | Iterable[str] | None. Let me know if that changes your process_priority plumbing.
